Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Fix resolv.conf detection

Some systems have other DNS servers along with 127.0.0.53 in /etc/resolv.conf
removed_lines: -			grep -v '^#\|^;' "$resolv_conf" | grep '^nameserver' | grep -v '127.0.0.53' | grep -oE '[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{1,3}){3}' | while read line; do
added_lines: +			grep -v '^#\|^;' "$resolv_conf" | grep '^nameserver' | grep -oE '[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{1,3}){3}' | while read line; do

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Add support for AlmaLinux and Rocky Linux

An unrelated fix to avoid one harmless warning during removal is also included.

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: resolv.conf parsing optimizations
removed_lines: -			grep -v '^#\|^;' "$resolv_conf" | grep '^nameserver' | grep -oE '[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{1,3}){3}' | while read line; do
added_lines: +			grep -v '#' "$resolv_conf" | grep nameserver | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{1,3}){3}' | while read line; do

Nyr/openvpn-install.message: egrep IP regex optimizations

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Addresses #694

- Use a checkip service which works fine over HTTP to avoid issues in systems
where ca-certificates is not available
- Increase timeout to 10 seconds, because the new service is a bit slower from
some locations
- Improve grep sanitization

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: Small style improvements
removed_lines: -			grep -v '#' "$resolv_conf" | grep nameserver | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do
-					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
-						ip6=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv6 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64 '"'"'!'"'"' -d fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
-				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
added_lines: +					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
+						ip6=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv6 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64 '"'"'!'"'"' -d fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
+				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
+		grep -v '#' "$resolv_conf" | grep nameserver | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
IPv6 support

Clients will be provided with IPv6 connectivity if the server has it.

Other very small and unimportant improvements are also included in this commit.
removed_lines: -					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
-						ip6=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv6 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64 '"'"'!'"'"' -d fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
-		ip6=$(ip -6 addr | grep 'inet6 [23]' | cut -d '/' -f 1 | grep -oE '([0-9a-fA-F]{0,4}:){1,7}[0-9a-fA-F]{0,4}' | sed -n "$ip6_number"p)
added_lines: +					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 10)
+		number_of_ips=$(ip addr | grep inet | grep -v inet6 | grep -vEc '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}')

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Miscellaneous improvements

This commit contains lots changes which are not very significant on its own but
provide important usability improvements and future proofing.

It also includes changes which required OpenVPN v2.4+ and were pending until
that version became widely available.

- General cleanup
- Improved IP address and NAT configuration
- Added input validation and sanitization
- Fix #603
- Remove "sndbuf" and "recvbuf" parameters
- Add server-side "explicit-exit-notify"
- Switch from "setenv opt" to "ignore-unknown-option"
- Switch from "tls-auth" to "tls-crypt"
- Other minor bugfixes and optimizations
removed_lines: -					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 10)
-				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
-		number_of_ips=$(ip addr | grep inet | grep -v inet6 | grep -vEc '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}')
-		grep -v '#' "$resolv_conf" | grep nameserver | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do
-	if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
added_lines: +					IP=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 10)
+				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$PORT" != '1194' ]]; then
+		grep -v '#' $RESOLVCONF | grep 'nameserver' | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do
+	if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$PORT" != '1194' ]]; then

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Migrate to the new systemd service

OpenVPN 2.4 packages provide a new systemd service unit which uses a different
directory structure. This commit drops support for Ubuntu 16.04 which has v2.3
packages.

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Revert "Improve iptables configuration"

This reverts commit fdc2bfbdace91a8e456f9623c0d9215978f7d888.
added_lines: +echo "iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s 10.8.0.0/24 ! -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to $IP || iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -s 10.8.0.0/24 ! -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to $IP" >> /etc/openvpn/iptables-init
+			echo "iptables -C INPUT -p $PROTOCOL --dport $PORT -j ACCEPT || iptables -I INPUT -p $PROTOCOL --dport $PORT -j ACCEPT

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Improve iptables configuration

See #464.
removed_lines: -echo "iptables -t nat -C POSTROUTING -s 10.8.0.0/24 ! -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to $IP || iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -s 10.8.0.0/24 ! -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to $IP" >> /etc/openvpn/iptables-init
-			echo "iptables -C INPUT -p $PROTOCOL --dport $PORT -j ACCEPT || iptables -I INPUT -p $PROTOCOL --dport $PORT -j ACCEPT

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Cleanup

- SELinux in CentOS already has rules for both udp/1194 and tcp/1194,
so the protocol check was not needed.
- Remove unneeded arguments from some grep and rm commands.
removed_lines: -				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$PORT" != '1194' ]]; then
-	if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$PORT" != '1194' ]]; then
added_lines: +					if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then
+		if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Remove IP address detection fallback

It was never used, the one-liner is enough.

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: Fix system resolvers option for environments running systemd-resolved
removed_lines: -		grep -v '#' $RESOLVCONF | grep 'nameserver' | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do
added_lines: +		grep -v '#' /etc/resolv.conf | grep 'nameserver' | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Debian 9 compatibility and small bug fixes

- Removed Debian 9 compatibility warning
- openvpn-blacklist is no longer uninstalled on removal
- Improvement: removal of /usr/share/doc/openvpn* hasn't been needed
for years
- Fixed: live iptables removal was failing for Debian since
6d51476047d6d7a610f292f9bbd6da75d2d8f96e

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Enable internal networking

See #299.
removed_lines: -					IP=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 10)
added_lines: +					IP=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 7)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Multiple firewall bug fixes

- When FirewallD is detected, NAT is now applied via FirewallD instead
of iptables (fixes #267).
- iptables REJECT/DROP/ACCEPT rules where not being properly detected.
- iptables rules were applied even when FirewallD was detected and the
same rules were being applied via firewall-cmd.
removed_lines: -					IP=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 -j SNAT --to ' | cut -d " " -f 7)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Improved iptables management

Rules are now instantly removed when uninstalling.

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Michael
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Changed iptables to not lookup hosts

Should be faster lookup on iptables if firewall rules contain lots
of host IP addresses (no need for a DNS lookup on each one!)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Avoid error message if sestatus isn't available

Just a cosmetic change.
removed_lines: -					if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then
-		if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then
added_lines: +				if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then
+	if sestatus | grep "Current mode" | grep -qs "enforcing"; then

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
The BIG commit

- Upgrade to easy-rsa 3.0.0
- Firewall support: rules are added for both FirewallD and iptables if
needed.
- Creation of our own configuration files for both the server and
clients.
- Using subnet topology instead of the deprecated net30.
- Removed port 53 question during install: user can just choose that
port during setup.
- Removed internal networking option: this is a road warrior installer
after all.
- Bugfix: the default easy-rsa directory was not correctly deleted if
one was already there.
added_lines: +	sed -i 's|;push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"|push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"|' server.conf

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Replaced Yandex DNS with Google

Yandex DNS is not stable enough, Google was previously missing.

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Multiple improvements

- Better UX for client certificate revocation: a list of the current
client names is shown to the user
- easy-rsa 2.2.2 now used by default: it’s easier for me to maintain a
single version

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: CentOS support and other improvements
added_lines: +IP=$(ifconfig | grep 'inet addr:' | grep -v inet6 | grep -vE '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}' | head -1)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: Options for custom DNS and intra-VPN connectivity

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Now using resolvers from resolv.conf

This will help with some ISPs restricting access to third party DNS
servers like it happens with LowEndSpirit and Torqhost.
removed_lines: -	cat /etc/resolv.conf | grep -v '#' | grep 'nameserver' | grep -E -o '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | while read line; do

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: Bugfix for systems with multiple IPv4 addresses available
removed_lines: -IP=$(ifconfig | grep 'inet addr:' | grep -v inet6 | grep -vE '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}' | head -1)
added_lines: +IP=$(ifconfig | grep 'inet addr:' | grep -v inet6 | grep -vE '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}')

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: 2048 bit keys by default and Debian Jessie compatibility
added_lines: +	sed -i 's|;push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"|push "redirect-gateway def1 bypass-dhcp"|' server.conf

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message:
Various bugfixes and improvements

- Assisted configuration for servers behind a NAT
- Better IP autodetection
- Fix certificate revocation
removed_lines: -IP=$(ifconfig | grep 'inet addr:' | grep -v inet6 | grep -vE '127\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' | cut -d: -f2 | awk '{ print $1}')
added_lines: +	IP=$(grep address /etc/network/interfaces | grep -v 127.0.0.1  | awk '{print $2}' | grep '.' | head -1)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: Bugfix: better IPv4 autodetection on some IPv6 enabled servers
removed_lines: -	IP=$(grep address /etc/network/interfaces | grep -v 127.0.0.1  | awk '{print $2}' | grep '.' | head -1)

Nyr/openvpn-install.author: Nyr
Nyr/openvpn-install.message: First commit

+		ip6=$(ip -6 addr | grep 'inet6 [23]' | cut -d '/' -f 1 | grep -oE '([0-9a-fA-F]{0,4}:){1,7}[0-9a-fA-F]{0,4}' | sed -n "$ip6_number"p)
+			grep -v '^#\|^;' "$resolv_conf" | grep '^nameserver' | grep -v '127.0.0.53' | grep -oE '[0-9]{1,3}(\.[0-9]{1,3}){3}' | while read line; do
+	if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
+					ip=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv4 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s 10.8.0.0/24 '"'"'!'"'"' -d 10.8.0.0/24' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
+						ip6=$(firewall-cmd --direct --get-rules ipv6 nat POSTROUTING | grep '\-s fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64 '"'"'!'"'"' -d fddd:1194:1194:1194::/64' | grep -oE '[^ ]+$')
+				if sestatus 2>/dev/null | grep "Current mode" | grep -q "enforcing" && [[ "$port" != 1194 ]]; then
